_note: Revised per QA FAIL report ba-qa-report-20260524-171714.json (F1-F7). All arch-6 hook ACs use nested stdin fixture shape {tool_name,tool_input:{prompt}}. Blocking exit code is eq/2. AC1 split into AC1a (files_modified) + AC1b (files_created). AC4 split into AC4a + AC4b. AC6 machine check verifies matcher co-location and executable bit. AC7 uses EISDIR deterministic harness. AC9 added for line-115 mtime-refresh guard.

acceptance_criteria.given: A dev-report JSON exists at docs/dev/dev-report-<task-id>.json with dev.files_modified containing a gitignored path (e.g., 'docs/some-doc.md' under docs/* rule) AND dev.files_created is empty AND no top-level gitignore_waiver field is present; the hook is invoked with stdin payload {"tool_name":"Agent","tool_input":{"prompt":"... Dev report file: docs/dev/dev-report-<task-id>.json ..."}}
acceptance_criteria.when: hooks/pretool-gitignore-preflight.py is invoked as a PreToolUse hook with the above stdin payload
acceptance_criteria.then: The hook exits with code 2 and writes a BLOCKED message to stderr naming the gitignored path

check.also: stderr contains 'BLOCKED' and the gitignored path name

acceptance_criteria.given: A dev-report JSON exists at docs/dev/dev-report-<task-id>.json with dev.files_created containing a gitignored path (e.g., 'docs/new-doc.md' under docs/* rule) AND dev.files_modified is empty AND no top-level gitignore_waiver field is present; the hook is invoked with stdin payload {"tool_name":"Agent","tool_input":{"prompt":"... Dev report file: docs/dev/dev-report-<task-id>.json ..."}}
acceptance_criteria.when: hooks/pretool-gitignore-preflight.py is invoked as a PreToolUse hook with the above stdin payload
acceptance_criteria.then: The hook exits with code 2 and writes a BLOCKED message to stderr naming the gitignored path

check.also: stderr contains 'BLOCKED' and the gitignored path name

acceptance_criteria.given: A dev-report JSON where all paths in dev.files_modified and dev.files_created are NOT matched by .gitignore (e.g., 'hooks/pretool-gitignore-preflight.py', 'settings.json'); invoked with stdin payload {"tool_name":"Agent","tool_input":{"prompt":"... Dev report file: docs/dev/dev-report-<task-id>.json ..."}}
acceptance_criteria.when: hooks/pretool-gitignore-preflight.py is invoked with the above stdin payload

acceptance_criteria.given: An Agent prompt JSON whose tool_input.prompt contains a dev-report path that does NOT exist on disk
acceptance_criteria.when: hooks/pretool-gitignore-preflight.py is invoked with stdin {"tool_name":"Agent","tool_input":{"prompt":"... Dev report file: docs/dev/dev-report-nonexistent-999.json ..."}}
acceptance_criteria.then: The hook exits 0 (no-op; does not block pre-dev Agent dispatches)

acceptance_criteria.given: A dev-report JSON where dev.files_modified contains a gitignored path AND dev.files_created is empty AND a top-level gitignore_waiver field is non-null and non-empty (e.g., {"gitignore_waiver": "tracked via explicit exception", "dev": {"files_modified": ["docs/some-doc.md"], "files_created": []}}); invoked with standard nested stdin fixture
acceptance_criteria.when: hooks/pretool-gitignore-preflight.py is invoked with the above dev-report and stdin payload
acceptance_criteria.then: The hook exits 0 (waiver accepted; no block)

acceptance_criteria.given: A dev-report JSON where dev.files_created contains a gitignored path AND dev.files_modified is empty AND a top-level gitignore_waiver field is non-null and non-empty (e.g., {"gitignore_waiver": "tracked via explicit exception", "dev": {"files_modified": [], "files_created": ["docs/new-doc.md"]}}); invoked with standard nested stdin fixture
acceptance_criteria.when: hooks/pretool-gitignore-preflight.py is invoked with the above dev-report and stdin payload
acceptance_criteria.then: The hook exits 0 (waiver accepted; no block)

acceptance_criteria.given: An Agent prompt JSON whose tool_input.prompt does NOT contain any 'docs/dev/dev-report-*.json' path reference
acceptance_criteria.when: hooks/pretool-gitignore-preflight.py is invoked with stdin {"tool_name":"Agent","tool_input":{"prompt":"Run the QA suite and check the results"}}
acceptance_criteria.then: The hook exits 0 (no-op; no dev-report path identifiable in prompt)

acceptance_criteria.given: The current settings.json and the filesystem at hooks/pretool-gitignore-preflight.py
acceptance_criteria.when: settings.json hooks.PreToolUse array is parsed and the hook file is inspected
acceptance_criteria.then: One PreToolUse entry exists whose matcher field equals or contains 'Agent' AND that same entry's hooks[] array contains a command string including 'pretool-gitignore-preflight.py'; AND hooks/pretool-gitignore-preflight.py exists on disk with executable bit set (mode & 0o111 != 0)

acceptance_criteria.given: hooks/userprompt-tmpfs-pressure.sh with the arch-3 patch applied; a fixed session_id 'test-enospc-ac7' is injected via stdin (so COUNTER_FILE derives to /tmp/claude-pressure-warn-test-enospc-ac7); that derived path is pre-created as a directory (EISDIR causes redirection failure even for root); no cat shim is provided so cat on the directory path fails and n defaults to 0 (fresh-session / increment branch under test); a df shim on PATH emits >75% for /tmp and /dev/shm to activate the pressure branch; cleanup trap is installed before any setup steps
acceptance_criteria.when: The hook is invoked with that stdin and the shimmed PATH
acceptance_criteria.then: The hook exits 0 AND stdout is empty AND stderr is empty

check.harness_recipe: tmpdir=$(mktemp -d); shim_df="$tmpdir/df"; printf '#!/bin/bash\necho "Use%"; echo "76%"; echo "77%"\n' > "$shim_df"; chmod +x "$shim_df"; COUNTER_FILE="/tmp/claude-pressure-warn-test-enospc-ac7"; mkdir -p "$COUNTER_FILE"; trap 'rm -rf "$COUNTER_FILE" "${COUNTER_FILE}.lock" "$tmpdir"' EXIT INT TERM; stdin_json='{"session_id":"test-enospc-ac7"}'; out=$(PATH="$tmpdir:$PATH" bash hooks/userprompt-tmpfs-pressure.sh <<< "$stdin_json" 2>/tmp/ac7_stderr); exit_code=$?; stderr_out=$(cat /tmp/ac7_stderr); rm -f /tmp/ac7_stderr

acceptance_criteria.given: hooks/userprompt-tmpfs-pressure.sh after the arch-3 patch is applied
acceptance_criteria.when: The file is read and syntax-checked
acceptance_criteria.then: The file contains 'if ! printf' at line ~118 (failure-aware counter-increment write) instead of bare 'echo $((n + 1)) >' AND 'bash -n hooks/userprompt-tmpfs-pressure.sh' exits 0

acceptance_criteria.given: hooks/userprompt-tmpfs-pressure.sh after the arch-3 patch is applied
acceptance_criteria.then: The mtime-refresh write at line ~115 is guarded: the file contains a line matching the pattern '\$COUNTER_FILE.*||' (the counter-file write followed by the ENOSPC error-handler branch '|| { printf skip; exit 0; }') AND bash -n exits 0

check.description: Matches the mtime-refresh write line (line ~115) that must end with || { printf 'skip'; exit 0; }. The pattern binds to $COUNTER_FILE to distinguish from other lines and || to require the error-handler. A bare write without the guard would not contain || after $COUNTER_FILE.

acceptance_criteria.given: hooks/userprompt-tmpfs-pressure.sh with the arch-3 patch applied; a fixed session_id 'test-enospc-ac7b' is injected via stdin (so COUNTER_FILE derives to /tmp/claude-pressure-warn-test-enospc-ac7b); that derived path is pre-created as a directory (EISDIR); a cat shim on PATH emits '3' (the RATE_LIMIT value) so that the rate-limit check fires and the mtime-refresh write at line ~115 is reached (saturated branch, not increment branch); a df shim on PATH emits >75% for /tmp and /dev/shm to activate the pressure branch; cleanup trap is installed before any setup steps
acceptance_criteria.when: The hook is invoked with that stdin and the shimmed PATH (cat shim + df shim)
acceptance_criteria.then: The hook exits 0 AND stdout is empty AND stderr is empty (mtime-refresh write fails under EISDIR but hook degrades gracefully via the || { printf 'skip'; exit 0; } guard)

check.harness_recipe:
tmpdir=$(mktemp -d); shim_df="$tmpdir/df"; shim_cat="$tmpdir/cat"; printf '#!/bin/bash
echo "Use%"; echo "76%"; echo "77%"
' > "$shim_df"; chmod +x "$shim_df"; printf '#!/bin/bash
echo 3
' > "$shim_cat"; chmod +x "$shim_cat"; COUNTER_FILE="/tmp/claude-pressure-warn-test-enospc-ac7b"; mkdir -p "$COUNTER_FILE"; trap 'rm -rf "$COUNTER_FILE" "${COUNTER_FILE}.lock" "$tmpdir"' EXIT INT TERM; stdin_json='{"session_id":"test-enospc-ac7b"}'; out=$(PATH="$tmpdir:$PATH" bash hooks/userprompt-tmpfs-pressure.sh <<< "$stdin_json" 2>/tmp/ac7b_stderr); exit_code=$?; stderr_out=$(cat /tmp/ac7b_stderr); rm -f /tmp/ac7b_stderr

acceptance_criteria.given: A dev-report JSON where dev.files_modified contains 'logs/lifecycle.jsonl' AND dev.files_created is empty AND no top-level gitignore_waiver field is present; logs/lifecycle.jsonl is caught by the logs/* blanket rule but excepted by !logs/lifecycle.jsonl in .gitignore — git check-ignore --no-index --quiet -- logs/lifecycle.jsonl returns exit code 1 (not ignored); invoked with standard nested stdin fixture
acceptance_criteria.when: hooks/pretool-gitignore-preflight.py is invoked with the above dev-report and stdin payload
acceptance_criteria.then: The hook exits 0 (negation exception applies; path is not gitignored; no block)

check.also: precondition: git check-ignore --no-index --quiet -- logs/lifecycle.jsonl returns rc=1 in this repo

acceptance_criteria.given: A dev-report JSON where dev.files_modified contains a gitignored path (e.g., 'docs/some-doc.md' under docs/* rule) AND a top-level gitignore_waiver field is present but set to "" (empty string) — e.g., {"gitignore_waiver": "", "dev": {"files_modified": ["docs/some-doc.md"], "files_created": []}}; invoked with standard nested stdin fixture
acceptance_criteria.when: hooks/pretool-gitignore-preflight.py is invoked with the above dev-report and stdin payload
acceptance_criteria.then: The hook exits with code 2 (empty-string waiver is not accepted; only a non-null, non-empty gitignore_waiver value grants exemption)

check.also: stderr contains 'BLOCKED' and the gitignored path name
